(formerly known as **Cardiff International Arena & Convention Centre** and **Motorpoint Arena Cardiff** and currently, for sponsorship reasons, as **Utilita Arena Cardiff**) is an indoor [exhibition centre](/wiki/Exhibition_centre "Exhibition centre") and events arena located in [Cardiff](/wiki/Cardiff "Cardiff"), Wales, and was opened on 9 September 1993 by singer [Shirley Bassey](/wiki/Shirley_Bassey "Shirley Bassey").[[1]](#cite_note-Opening-1) It is Cardiff's largest purpose-built exhibition facility and its former name was due to a sponsorship agreement from 2011 to 2022. The upstairs of the building is known as the World Trade Centre.[[2]](#cite_note-WTC-2)

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Cardiff+International+Arena%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Cardiff+International+Arena%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Cardiff+International+Arena%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Cardiff+International+Arena%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Motorpoint Arena Cardiff](/w/index.php?title=Motorpoint_Arena_Cardiff&redirect=no "Motorpoint Arena Cardiff"))

Indoor arena in Cardiff, Wales

This article is about the purpose-built exhibition facility. For the temporary ice arena, see [Cardiff Arena](/wiki/Cardiff_Arena "Cardiff Arena"). For the proposed indoor arena, see [New Cardiff Bay Arena](/wiki/New_Cardiff_Bay_Arena "New Cardiff Bay Arena").

Utilita Arena Cardiff

**Cardiff International Arena** (formerly known as **Cardiff International Arena & Convention Centre** and **Motorpoint Arena Cardiff** and currently, for sponsorship reasons, as **Utilita Arena Cardiff**) is an indoor [exhibition centre](/wiki/Exhibition_centre "Exhibition centre") and events arena located in [Cardiff](/wiki/Cardiff "Cardiff"), Wales, and was opened on 9 September 1993 by singer [Shirley Bassey](/wiki/Shirley_Bassey "Shirley Bassey").[[1]](#cite_note-Opening-1) It is Cardiff's largest purpose-built exhibition facility and its former name was due to a sponsorship agreement from 2011 to 2022. The upstairs of the building is known as the World Trade Centre.[[2]](#cite_note-WTC-2)

The arena contains a number of function areas, the largest being the main arena which has hosted many national and international events, such as concerts, sports and comedy performances.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Utilita_Arena_Cardiff_September_2023_09.jpg/250px-Utilita_Arena_Cardiff_September_2023_09.jpg)](/wiki/File:Utilita_Arena_Cardiff_September_2023_09.jpg)

The red dragon outside the main entrance to the arena in 2023

During the construction phase, the venue was known as the Cardiff World Trade Centre and was first expected to open in January 1992.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It formed part of a complex which included a new hotel built opposite that opened in 1990, the nearby [Wales National Ice Rink](/wiki/Wales_National_Ice_Rink "Wales National Ice Rink") and a number of surrounding office buildings.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The renamed arena was eventually opened on 9 September 1993, by [Shirley Bassey](/wiki/Shirley_Bassey "Shirley Bassey"), in front of 5,500 fans.[[1]](#cite_note-Opening-1) The concert was later televised on [BBC One](/wiki/BBC_One "BBC One") on 30 July 1994.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It was officially opened by [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II "Queen Elizabeth II") on 14 October 1993.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The original lease was granted to the developer [Brent Walker](/wiki/Brent_Walker "Brent Walker"), who subsequently assigned it to [The Ambassador Theatre Group](/wiki/ATG_Entertainment "ATG Entertainment"). In 1999, [SFX Entertainment](/wiki/SFX_Entertainment "SFX Entertainment") acquired the arena.[[7]](#cite_note-7) It is now owned by the US media giant [Live Nation UK](/wiki/Live_Nation_UK "Live Nation UK"), who have confirmed they would cease to operate the existing arena and terminate their leasehold interest in the council-owned site if the [new proposed 15,000-seat indoor arena](/wiki/New_Cardiff_Bay_Arena "New Cardiff Bay Arena") in [Atlantic Wharf](/wiki/Atlantic_Wharf "Atlantic Wharf") is built to replace it and are successful in securing the operator lease.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The CIA is then expected to be closed by Live Nation and could be demolished, along with the adjoining surface car parks and buildings owned by Rapport, to provide space for a new commercial development that would be integrated into the [Canal Quarter](/wiki/Bute_Docks_Feeder#Churchill_Way_Canal_Quarter "Bute Docks Feeder") on Churchill Way.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/3e/Cardiff_International_Arena.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cardiff_International_Arena.jpg)

The CIA logo used before the arena was rebranded under the Motorpoint sponsorship agreement.

On 1 March 2011, the Cardiff International Arena was renamed Motorpoint Arena Cardiff, for sponsorship reasons, after the car sales company entered into an agreement regarding the naming rights to the arena for five years in what was called "a seven-figure investment". Other names who have performed in the arena are among [Westlife](/wiki/Westlife "Westlife"), [Kylie Minogue](/wiki/Kylie_Minogue "Kylie Minogue"), [One Direction](/wiki/One_Direction "One Direction"), [Jessie J](/wiki/Jessie_J "Jessie J"), [Anastacia](/wiki/Anastacia "Anastacia"), [Steps](/wiki/Steps_(pop_group) "Steps (pop group)"), [JLS](/wiki/JLS "JLS"), [Jason Derulo](/wiki/Jason_Derulo "Jason Derulo") and [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey").[[10]](#cite_note-Naming-10)

In March 2015, the venue's 3000th event was marked by a show headlined by singer [Katherine Jenkins](/wiki/Katherine_Jenkins "Katherine Jenkins").[[11]](#cite_note-11) In July 2016, Motorpoint extended its naming rights contract with the arena until at least 2021. This included a complete re-branding of the building including installation of outdoor LED signage on the arena's towers.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Across 2017 and 2018, the arena underwent a significant 18-month upgrade and refurbishment funded by Live Nation in time for its 25th anniversary; this included installation of a new bar by [Carlsberg](/wiki/Carlsberg_Group "Carlsberg Group") called The Danish Quarter, refurbishment of the smaller Exit 7 space and existing L2 Restaurant & Bar, updated hospitality suites and [hearing loop](/wiki/Hearing_loop "Hearing loop") system, as well as new lifts, facial recognition cameras and other security related upgrades.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The arena reverted back to its original CIA name on 22 September 2022 after being named Motorpoint Arena Cardiff since March 2011.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

During August 2023 the arena was renamed Utilita Arena Cardiff, for sponsorship reasons, after the energy company entered into an agreement regarding the naming rights to the arena.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Facilities")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Inside_Cardiff_International_Arena_%282022%29.jpg/250px-Inside_Cardiff_International_Arena_%282022%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Inside_Cardiff_International_Arena_(2022).jpg)

Inside the Cardiff International Arena in October 2022

The main arena offers 4,500 square metres of exhibition space, and is very versatile, for the number of different events that take place at the arena. Full capacity can reach 7,500, in a standing layout and 5,000, for a fully seated event.

There are over 30 additional areas, including a conference suite (groups of up to 460) and four executive boardrooms.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Events")]

### Comedy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Comedy")]

Comedian [Lee Evans](/wiki/Lee_Evans_(comedian) "Lee Evans (comedian)") has brought four tours XL Tour, Big, Roadrunner and Monsters to the arena, Evans played his last show before retiring from stand-up at the arena from 24 to 30 November 2014.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Music")]

### Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Sport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9c/Project_365_-51_200215_Snooker_Loopy%21_%2816569505646%29.jpg/250px-Project_365_-51_200215_Snooker_Loopy%21_%2816569505646%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Project_365_-51_200215_Snooker_Loopy!_(16569505646).jpg)

Arena hosting the [Welsh Open](/wiki/Welsh_Open_(snooker) "Welsh Open (snooker)") [snooker](/wiki/Snooker "Snooker") championship in 2015

In 1999, the Cardiff International Arena hosted the ([Nine-ball](/wiki/Nine-ball "Nine-ball")) [World Professional Pool Championship](/wiki/World_Professional_Pool_Championship "World Professional Pool Championship"). The official [WPA World Nine-ball Championship](/wiki/List_of_WPA_World_Nine-ball_champions "List of WPA World Nine-ball champions") was hosted at the Cardiff International Arena between 2000 and 2003.

Since 2007, the arena has been one of the [Premier League Darts](/wiki/Premier_League_Darts "Premier League Darts") venues, including the semi-finals and finals night of the [2008 Premier League Darts](/wiki/2008_Premier_League_Darts "2008 Premier League Darts") on 26 May 2008. The [PDC](/wiki/Professional_Darts_Corporation "Professional Darts Corporation") [Champions League of Darts](/wiki/Champions_League_of_Darts "Champions League of Darts") was held in the arena in 2016 and 2017.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

In February 2015, the [Welsh Open](/wiki/Welsh_Open_(snooker) "Welsh Open (snooker)") [snooker](/wiki/Snooker "Snooker") championship was transferred from the [Newport Centre](/wiki/Newport_Centre_(Wales) "Newport Centre (Wales)") (where it had taken place since 2005) to the arena.[[21]](#cite_note-21) The arena previously hosted the championship between 1999 and 2003. In [2021](/wiki/2021_Welsh_Open_(snooker) "2021 Welsh Open (snooker)") the event was transferred back to Newport, this time held at the [Celtic Manor Resort](/wiki/Celtic_Manor_Resort "Celtic Manor Resort").

### Professional wrestling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cardiff_International_Arena&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Professional wrestling")]

In 2019, [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE")'s [NXT UK](/wiki/NXT_UK_(WWE_brand) "NXT UK (WWE brand)") brand announced the venue would host the second ever TakeOver event for the brand, [NXT UK TakeOver: Cardiff](/wiki/NXT_UK_TakeOver:_Cardiff "NXT UK TakeOver: Cardiff"). This took place on 31 August 2019 and was the first TakeOver to take place in Wales. On 21 August 2024, [All Elite Wrestling](/wiki/All_Elite_Wrestling "All Elite Wrestling") made their debut in Wales, airing *[Dynamite](/wiki/AEW_Dynamite "AEW Dynamite")* and *[Collision](/wiki/AEW_Collision "AEW Collision")* as part of [All In London](/wiki/All_In_(2024) "All In (2024)") week.[[22]](#cite_note-22) At the [2025 Forbidden Door](/wiki/Forbidden_Door_(2025) "Forbidden Door (2025)"), AEW announced their return to the venue for *Collision* on 13 December 2025.[[23]](#cite_note-23)
